The branch updates cron isolated-agent finalization to derive persisted session totalTokens from agentMeta.lastCallUsage while keeping aggregate telemetry counters and adding a focused harness regression test.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +63. Total +65 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main passes aggregate cron run usage into deriveSessionTotalTokens, and the linked report includes a focused harness reproduction. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.

target.title: [codex] fix cron session total token accounting
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91716.

- Use the final API call usage (`lastCallUsage`) for the persisted cron session `totalTokens` context snapshot, falling back to aggregate usage when final-call usage is unavailable.
- Keep aggregate run usage for input/output/cache counters and estimated cost.
- Add focused cron isolated-agent regression coverage for mixed aggregate and final-call usage.

## Root cause

`finalizeCronRun` passed cumulative `agentMeta.usage` into `deriveSessionTotalTokens`, so a cron run with interim retries could persist cumulative run tokens as the session context snapshot instead of the final request's context size.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.usage-accounting.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.test-harness.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.usage-accounting.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /private/tmp/codex-autoreview-service-tier-fast` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cron session token snapshots now use final-call context usage instead of cumulative run usage while preserving aggregate telemetry counters.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw PR worktree rebased on `origin/main` (`61e93a800ddd9e9d7fb94fd2464dcfa461834dfd`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.usage-accounting.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: The regression test asserts `inputTokens`/`outputTokens` remain aggregate values and `deriveSessionTotalTokens` receives `lastCallUsage` for `totalTokens`.

Observed result after fix: Focused Vitest shard passed: 1 file, 1 test.

What was not tested: Full suite and GitHub CI; this is a narrow cron accounting patch and CI can run on the draft PR.
